A rectangle has side lengths in the ratio of $2:5$. If all of its sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the resulting rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $3:7$. 

*highlighted section* The original side lengths can be represented as $2x$ and $5x$, where $x$ is a common factor. When extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. 

*highlighted section* The new ratio is given as $3:7$, so we can set up the equation:  
$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$

*highlighted section* Solving this equation, we cross-multiply:  
$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9)
$$

*highlighted section* Expanding both sides:  
$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$

*highlighted section* Subtracting $14x$ from both sides:  
$$
63 = x + 27
$$

*highlighted section* Subtracting $27$ from both sides:  
$$
x = 36
$$

*highlighted section* Therefore, the original side lengths are $2x = 72 \text{ cm}$ and $5x = 180 \text{ cm}$. 

*highlighted section* When extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths are $72 + 9 = 81 \text{ cm}$ and $180 + 9 = 189 \text{ cm}$. 

*highlighted section* The new ratio is $81:189$, which simplifies to $3:7$ as given. 

*highlighted section* If we extend all sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths will be $81 + 9 = 90 \text{ cm}$ and $189 + 9 = 198 \text{ cm}$. 

*highlighted section* The new ratio is $90:198$, which simplifies to $5:11$.
